lede: A look at December 6 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Deborah Estrin and Richard Edlund, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Donald Trump
events.desc: Donald Trump's administration officially announces the recognition of Jerusalem as the capital of Israel.

events.title: Babri Masjid
events.desc: The Babri Masjid in Ayodhya, India, is demolished, leading to widespread riots causing the death of over 1,500 people.

events.title: Italian Air Force
events.desc: A military jet of the Italian Air Force, abandoned by its pilot after an on-board fire, crashed into a high school near Bologna, Italy, killing 12 students and injuring 88 other people.

events.title: Adrian Kantrowitz
events.desc: Adrian Kantrowitz performs the first human heart transplant in the United States.

events.title: Blood in the Water match
events.desc: A violent water polo match between Hungary and the USSR takes place during the 1956 Summer Olympics in Melbourne, against the backdrop of the Hungarian Revolution of 1956.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Central Powers capture Bucharest.

events.title: Theodore Roosevelt
events.desc: Theodore Roosevelt articulated his "Corollary" to the Monroe Doctrine, stating that the U.S. would intervene in the Western Hemisphere should Latin American governments prove incapable or unstable.

events.title: Mongol invasion of Kievan Rus'
events.desc: Mongol invasion of Rus': Kyiv, defended by Voivode Dmytro, falls to the Mongols under Batu Khan.

events.title: Béla I of Hungary
events.desc: Béla I is crowned king of Hungary.

births.name: Richard Edlund
births.line: American visual effects designer and cinematographer

births.name: Andrew Cuomo
births.line: American politician, 56th Governor of New York

births.name: Peter Handke
births.line: Austrian author and playwright, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Jean-Paul Ngoupandé
births.line: Central African politician, Prime Minister of the Central African Republic

deaths.text: Ralph H. Baer, German-American video game designer, created the Magnavox Odyssey
João Goulart, Brazilian lawyer and politician, 24th President of Brazil
Werner von Siemens, German engineer and businessman, founded the Siemens Company

observances.text: Christian feast day: María del Monte Carmelo Sallés y Barangueras
Christian feast day: Nicholas of Myra, and its related observances: Saint Nicholas Day, where Saint Nicholas/Santa Claus leaves little presents in children's shoes. (International)
